body:
## Context

After #181 ships, admins can cancel or modify individual occurrences of recurring events. But parishioners won't know unless they check the calendar. If Sunday Qurbono is cancelled or moved to 10 AM for Easter, people need to be told — not left to discover it when they show up at 9:15.

## What to do

### Trigger notifications on occurrence changes

When an admin saves a change via the #181 UI, fire an email notification:

**Occurrence cancelled:**
- Subject: "⚠️ [Event Name] on [Date] has been cancelled"
- Body: event name, original date/time, cancellation reason, next occurrence date
- e.g. "Bible Study on Wednesday, April 15 has been cancelled. Reason: No Bible Study during Holy Week. The next Bible Study is Wednesday, April 22 at 7:00 PM."

**Occurrence modified (time/location changed):**
- Subject: "📅 [Event Name] on [Date] — schedule change"
- Body: event name, date, what changed (old → new), any note from the admin
- e.g. "Holy Qurbono on Easter Sunday, April 19 has been updated. Start time changed from 9:15 AM to 10:00 AM."

**Occurrence restored (un-cancelled):**
- Subject: "✅ [Event Name] on [Date] is back on"
- Body: event name, date/time, confirmation it's happening as scheduled

### Who gets notified

Option A: All confirmed email subscribers (`email_subscribers` where `confirmed = true`)
Option B: Only members (profiles with role = 'member')
Option C: Admin chooses at send time (checkbox: "Notify subscribers about this change")

**Recommendation:** Option C — let the admin decide. A minor time tweak might not warrant an email blast, but a cancellation should. Show a checkbox in the #181 modal: "Send notification to subscribers" (default checked for cancellations, unchecked for edits).

### Implementation

- React Email template: `EventChangeEmail.tsx`
- Use Resend (already integrated, free tier covers this volume)
- Server action: after writing to `event_instances`, check the notification flag, query subscribers, send batch email
- Rate limit: batch via Resend's batch API to stay within free tier limits

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Cancelling an occurrence with "notify" checked sends email to subscribers
- [ ] Modifying an occurrence with "notify" checked sends email with change details
- [ ] Restoring an occurrence with "notify" checked sends "back on" email
- [ ] Email includes the event name, date, what changed, and next occurrence
- [ ] Admin can opt out of sending notification (uncheck the box)
- [ ] Uses existing Resend infrastructure and React Email templates

## Dependencies

- #181 (recurring event exceptions UI — this adds the notification layer on top)
- Existing Resend + React Email setup
- `email_subscribers` table (already exists)
title: Notify subscribers when a recurring event is cancelled or modified
